missing_info: show ip route on R1 (or at minimum 'show ip route 172.16.0.0') — to confirm R1 has a route to 172.16.0.0/24 and next-hop.
show ip route on R2 (or at minimum 'show ip route 192.168.0.0') — to confirm return path from R2 to 192.168.0.0/24.
show ip ospf neighbor (on R1, R2, R3) — to verify OSPF adjacencies are established between the routers.
show ip interface brief (and/or 'show interfaces Serial0/1/0' and Serial0/1/1) on R1 and R2 — to confirm the serial link interfaces and LAN facing interfaces are up/up and no line/protocol down.
show access-lists (with hit counts) on R1 and R2 — to check whether R1_TO_R2 or R2_TO_R1 ACLs are matching traffic (ICMP) and if any ACEs are denying packets.
show ip arp 192.168.0.12 on R1 and show ip arp 172.16.0.12 on R2 (or equivalent host ARP/neighbor info) — to confirm the hosts are reachable at L2 from their respective routers.

From the network side: traceroute from R1 to 172.16.0.12 (and/or traceroute from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12) and ping from R1 to 172.16.0.12 — to see where probes stop and whether the issue is routing, forwarding, or host-level.

Provide R1 and R2 interface operational state for Serial0/1/0 and the LAN interfaces (e.g., output of 'show ip interface brief' or 'show interfaces Serial0/1/0' and 'show interfaces GigabitEthernet0/0/0' on both routers) to confirm the link and LAN interfaces are up/up.

Provide the active routing tables on R1 and R2 (output of 'show ip route') to confirm the static routes to 172.16.0.0/24 and 192.168.0.0/24 are installed in the RIB and there are no conflicting routes.

Provide ACL hit counters for R1_TO_R2 and R2_TO_R1 (output of 'show access-lists R1_TO_R2' and 'show access-lists R2_TO_R1') to determine whether packets are being matched/denied by the ACLs applied outbound on the R1-R2 serial link.

Provide basic host-level info for the endpoints 192.168.0.12 and 172.16.0.12: configured default gateway and host firewall status (e.g., 'ipconfig /all' or 'ip addr' plus 'route -n' / 'ip route show'), and a traceroute from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 (or packet capture) to show where ICMP is being dropped.

plan_history.intent_description: Assess network reachability failure: source 192.168.0.12 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.12 (25 pings sent, 0 replies). Goal: identify root cause and restore bidirectional IP routing so hosts in 192.168.0.0/24 and 172.16.0.0/24 can reach each other.
plan_history.problem: No IP-level connectivity from 192.168.0.12 to 172.16.0.12 (25/25 pings failed). Likely missing or incorrect routes between the 192.168.0.0/24 and 172.16.0.0/24 networks.
plan_history.fix_summary: Add reciprocal static routes on the edge routers to ensure 192.168.0.0/24 and 172.16.0.0/24 have explicit next-hops. Assumed next-hops: router1 -> router2 = 10.0.0.2; router2 -> router1 = 10.0.0.1. Adjust next-hops if actual interface addresses differ.

parameters: note=Assumed next-hop; verify actual peer address before applying if known
note=Assumed next-hop; verify actual peer address before applying if known

plan_history.intent_description: Assess network reachability failure: source 192.168.0.12 cannot reach destination 172.16.0.12 (25 pings sent, 0 replies). Goal: identify root cause and restore bidirectional IP routing so hosts in 192.168.0.0/24 and 172.16.0.0/24 can reach each other.
plan_history.problem: ICMP from 192.168.0.0/24 to 172.16.0.0/24 is failing (25/25 pings lost) after static routes were added on both routers; likely ACLs applied to the R1-R2 serial link are denying ICMP.
plan_history.fix_summary: Allow ICMP between the two subnets by updating the R1_TO_R2 and R2_TO_R1 ACLs to permit ICMP for the subnet pairs.

parameters: reason: allow echo and echo-reply between subnets
reason: allow echo and echo-reply between subnets
